title: codex-claude v1.8.4 — survivable long reviews, closed coverage gaps, narrowed hook

context.why: v1.8.3 shipped and deployed, but three problems remained, all observed live: long Codex turns were still killed by a fixed per-wait cap; three test-coverage gaps were knowingly left open; and the review-findings hook misfired on every offline test run.

root_cause_found.finding: spec:763's tests could never boot a daemon under a redirected HOME.
root_cause_found.cause: macOS caps Unix socket paths at 104 bytes. The socket name `daemon-<pid>-<Date.now()>.sock` (31 chars) pushed a temp-HOME path to 109, so bind() failed INSIDE the stdio-ignored detached child and the parent could only report 'daemon did not come up'. The real HOME path is 56 bytes, which is why this never showed in normal use.
root_cause_found.fix: Shortened to `d-<pid>-<base36>.sock` (98 bytes under temp HOME, verified), added a fail-fast length guard in daemon._listen, and gave the child a boot-error channel (`<socket>.err`) so its real failure reaches the parent.

phases.name: Survivable long turns
changes: lib/drive-loop.mjs: new `onWaitExpiry: 'interrupt' | 'rewait'` (default interrupt). In rewait mode an expired per-wait cap logs and polls again instead of killing the turn; deadlineMs exhaustion STILL interrupts, so an explicit total budget is never weakened.
review-round.mjs / plan-round.mjs: pass onWaitExpiry 'rewait', and now honour testWaitMs() so the path is reachable offline (previously only commit-review-round read the seam).
lib/drive-loop.mjs: the allow-arm log names the COMMAND again ('approving safe command: <cmd>'); the 1.8.3 migration had reduced it to the method.

agents/codex-impl-reviewer.md + agents/codex-architect.md: documented the owned-session fallback for a turn that outlives the ~10-min Bash cap (start --private -> send/plan -> repeated wait in SEPARATE Bash calls -> read --out -> stop), scoped as the one sanctioned alternative and only after the bounded driver was killed.
SKILL.md: troubleshooting now states a wait timeout is a poll result, not a stopped turn.
tests: drive-loop: rewait polls and never interrupts; rewait still honours deadlineMs
round-scripts: SLOWTURN completes under a 400ms cap (was a timeout)

phases.name: Closed the three coverage gaps
changes: mock-appserver: APPROVESAFE (pytest -q — the one family safe-command approves), SLOWTURN, and static-reask branches keyed on the drivers' own re-ask phrases.

round-scripts tests: allow arm for BOTH drivers, review re-ask now asserts PARSED_VERDICT (it previously passed on UNCLEAR), plan-round deny -> re-ask -> plan persisted, plan-round allow without re-ask.

detached-cli: spec:763 closed for real — the precedence test drives the STATE path (non-private start, then corrupt only state.cwd), because a --socket variant is vacuous by construction (bin sets state=null when --socket is given). Verified: reversing the precedence now fails the test, where the first attempt still passed.
detached-cli: over-long socket path fails loudly with a named cause.

phases.name: Narrowed the review-findings hook (user-level)
changes: ~/.claude/hooks/enforce-receiving-code-review.sh exempts offline runs by COMMAND (CODEX_DRIVE_TEST_MODE, mock-appserver, node --test, npm test) rather than by output. Verified across 6 cases: tests exempt, real reviews still exit 2, clean passes still exit 0.

verification.suite: cd codex-claude && npm test — 228 pass / 0 fail / 3 skipped
verification.revert_checks: rewait (unit + driver), safe-approve allow arm, socket-length guard, and the read --out precedence test were each verified to FAIL when their fix is reverted
verification.leakage: temp-dir and mock-process delta 0 across a full run
verification.live: deployed-plugin end-to-end review of this diff is the final step

accepted_limits: In-process rewait cannot beat the Bash tool cap (~10 min per call); that is why the agent-md fallback exists.
commit-review-round keeps its hard total deadline — a shell gate must fail fast.
